response.justification: Bogdan brings 15+ years of broad software engineering experience and recent MCP/agentic-workflow exposure at Naver, but falls significantly short of the role's core requirements. Python — the dominant language for GenAI tooling — is absent from his skill set entirely. There is no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or named multi-agent frameworks anywhere in the resume. His AI-specific work appears confined to his current role, which began in April 2025, nowhere near the required 8 years of production-grade AI experience. The profile is that of a strong generalist engineer making an early pivot into AI, not a staff-level GenAI practitioner ready to lead enterprise customer engagements.
strengths: Direct Model Context Protocol (MCP) implementation experience at Naver maps to a listed key technology in the job description
15+ years of polyglot, full-stack delivery across cloud (AWS CDK), data-intensive systems, and embedded platforms demonstrates strong technical adaptability
Staff-level roles at multiple companies with evidence of mentorship, public talks, and open-source framework authorship
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the stated skill set, despite being the primary language for GenAI tooling, data pipelines, and the ecosystem at large
No demonstrated experience with RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation, or observability frameworks — all explicitly required
Production AI experience appears limited to the current role (April 2025), far short of the stated 8-year minimum; the pivot into GenAI is very recent
